# CineSmithPro

> Save the hours. Make the cut. An editor's companion that lives inside Adobe Premiere Pro. AI does the scrubbing, searching, and rough assembly. The editor stays in the creative seat. Two seats per subscription — editor in Premiere, EP on the web — both at once. Waitlist open.

CineSmithPro is a forthcoming Adobe Premiere Pro panel from CineArt AI Limited (London), and a companion to CineSmith (cinesmith.io). Same company, separate product, separate brand expression. Designed for working editors in post-production — documentary, drama, podcast, long-form — not the creator-economy / TikTok cohort.

## Positioning

- Lives **inside Adobe Premiere Pro** as a native panel — beside the timeline, not in a browser tab.
- **No round-trips, no exports, no second timeline.** The project never leaves Adobe.
- **Two seats per subscription** — the editor in Premiere, the EP/assistant in a web companion. They work in parallel on the same bin, transcripts, and project.
- **Built by editors for editors.** Two decades of editorial credits, multi-broadcaster delivery, Sundance and Cannes selections, a Grierson-recognised feature.

## The promise (the hours back)

- **Find the line in seconds, not 90 minutes.** Every clip transcribed and speaker-labelled on ingest. Type a phrase or a feeling, get every variant across every take with in/out points ready.
- **Hand off the rough.** Brief the panel ("32-min episode, intro + 3 acts, leave room for music beds"); it pulls selects, sequences them, lays a placeholder edit you shape.
- **EP works alongside, not after.** Web companion (no Premiere licence required) lets the EP pull tomorrow's selects, flag clips, drop voice-over notes — lands back in the panel ready to cut.
- **More than search.** A creative arsenal threaded into the edit, not bolted on the side (see Arsenal below).

## The Arsenal (AI capabilities, all inside the panel)

1. **Video extension** — extend a shot past its captured frame, generatively, into the timeline.
2. **Voice cloning** — patch a flubbed word, drop a clean re-read, fill the ADR gap. Speaker-faithful, frame-accurate.
3. **Image generation** — concept boards, lookups, stills, references — generated to brief, dropped into the bin labelled and project-tagged.
4. **Sync-cut assembly** — multicam aligned, takes locked, dialogue snapped to script, music bed laid in.
5. **Best sheet** — hand it every transcript in the project, get back a ranked best-of with timecodes and emotional context.
6. **Deep find** — semantic search beyond words. "The moment she gets emotional." "The bit that breaks the tension."
7. **Video generation** — net-new shots from a prompt or a still. Pickups, inserts, cutaways.
8. **Footage restoration** — denoise, deflicker, upscale, stabilise. Archive-grade.
9. **Auto reframe** — one cut, every aspect. 16:9 → 9:16 / 1:1, subject-tracked, never just letterboxed.

## Who it is for

- Documentary editors (90-minute interview → 22-minute cut)
- Drama series editors (multicam, long form)
- Podcast editors (talking head, social cutdowns)
- Post houses and studios (managed tier, SSO, seat-scale)

## What it is not

- Not auto-edit. The creative seat stays the editor's.
- Not a separate website. The panel mounts inside Premiere.
- Not a creator-tier toy. Built for working editors with doc/drama/long-form briefs.
- Not priced per head. One subscription = two seats (editor + EP).

## Status

Pre-launch, v0.7 beta. Waitlist open at https://cinesmithpro.com. The first fifty working editors with active doc, drama, or podcast briefs shape the platform — they get prioritised access. Pricing announced at launch. Studios and post houses managed tier from Q3 2026.

## Founder & maker

CineSmithPro is built by **Taimur Khan**, Founder of CineArt AI Limited. Full profile: https://www.cineartai.com/taimur-khan/

Taimur is the editor this tool was built for — which is also why it exists. Two careers run in parallel:

**Filmmaker — 20+ years in editorial.** Series editor and director credits across documentary, drama, commercial, and broadcast. Editorial credits with **Netflix, BBC, Channel 4, HBO, Discovery, Sky, Al Jazeera, Arte France, CNN, and The Economist**. Recognition at **Sundance and Cannes**; a **Grierson Award-winning** film. Notable cuts include *Jinnah* (Christopher Lee), *The Bengali Detective*, *World's Toughest Forces*, *Johnny vs Amber* (Discovery+), *The Russell Murders* (Sky Documentaries), and *Democracy in Flames*. Founded WIDEI Films at Ealing Studios in 2004.

**Technologist — software for the people who make screen work.** In **2009** launched **Skylight** (skylightit.com), a cloud SaaS for project management, tasks, time billing, and invoicing — covered by BBC News, The Guardian, and the FT. In **2017** joined **Outlier Ventures**, working on *Money Circles*, *Buy.co*, and *OpenData* — fintech, public-sector procurement, and big-data projects built on blockchain and ML/AI. Today, leads the **AI automation, agentic workflow design, and prompt engineering** work powering CineArt AI's products (CineSmith and CineSmithPro). Computer pioneer since the ZX Spectrum / Commodore 64 days. Holds an active pilot's licence.

**Entrepreneurial.** A consistent track record of building companies across screen and software — WIDEI Films (Ealing Studios, 2004), Blueberry Creative (TV/film talent agency, scaled to £7m+ turnover), Skylightit.com (2009), Skylight Interactive (digital operations and process automation on AWS for clients including UK ports, AMEY, Royal Mail), Creative Development Academy (emerging-filmmaker training with the Documentary Filmmakers Group, Rose Bruford College, and the Royal Film Commission of Jordan), CineArt AI (2024), and CineSmith / CineSmithPro (current).

This is the reason CineSmithPro isn't a wrapper. The pipeline knowledge from the cutting room and the engineering hands of a working technologist sit in the same person.
